description: PR5 commit 5 — chain-search trace emission (CHAIN_ROTATED). Three shared-student sections form a bridge-graph (CS101↔CS102↔CS103, no CS101↔CS103 overlap) so CS102 is the sole hotspot when all three are seeded at the same slot via ORM. chain_local_search's _find_overlap_partners excludes other hotspot courses, so CS102's partners = {CS101, CS103}; the search then picks a chain-2 that relocates CS102 + one partner to resolve the triple-clash. Test calls optimise_current_timetable(run_local_search=False, run_chain_search=True, run_cpsat_polish=False) on the seeded state so chain is the ONLY stage with a chance to move anything.
notes: Share graph: ST001-5 in CS101 and CS102; ST006-10 in CS102 and CS103. No shared students between CS101 and CS103.
Test seeds all 3 sections at SUN 08:00 via ORM → CS102 students can't place (all clash); CS102 becomes unique hotspot.
chain-2 partners = {CS101, CS103} (neither is hotspot once the greedy-evaluator places their cohorts on their own slots or alternative patterns).
Chain rotation: move CS102 off SUN AND move CS101 or CS103 off SUN → resolves all student clashes, lex score drops, chain accepted.
